meta.title: STEPSS — paediatric status epilepticus severity score
meta.authors: Sidharth, Sankhyan N, Malhi P, Jayashree M
meta.outcome: Risk of an unfavourable short-term outcome (Paediatric Overall Performance Category ≥3, i.e. moderate disability to death, at discharge) in children with status epilepticus.
meta.source_note: STEPSS: Sidharth et al., Seizure 2019 (doi 10.1016/j.seizure.2019.09.005, PMID 31536850) — the paediatric modification of STESS (Rossetti 2008), differing only in the age criterion (<2 years = 2 points instead of ≥65 years). Externally validated (Soydan Ekin 2022 AUC 0.92; Fetta 2024).
meta.cff_info:
What this is. STEPSS (Status Epilepticus in Pediatric patients Severity Score) is the paediatric version of STESS. It predicts an unfavourable short-term outcome — Paediatric Overall Performance Category (POPC) ≥3, i.e. at least moderate disability, up to death — at discharge in children with status epilepticus.

How it is scored. Four bedside items are added — level of consciousness (0–1), worst seizure type (0–2), age (<2 years = 2, ≥2 years = 0), and history of previous seizures (0–1) — for a total of 0–6. Only the age rule differs from the adult STESS.

What the score means (Sidharth 2019, 140 children). Discrimination was excellent (AUC 0.95). The optimal cut-off is >3 (i.e. ≥4): sensitivity 93%, specificity 81% for a poor outcome, with a very high negative predictive value (99%) — a score of 0–3 reliably identifies children who will do well. Positive predictive value is lower and rises with the score (≈37% at ≥4, ≈91% at ≥5).

Caveats. Single-centre derivation (New Delhi); the per-score figures shown are derived from the published cut-off operating characteristics (Table 3), not exact per-point risks, so read them as bands. Children 1 month–18 years. Population-level estimate — apply clinical judgement.

predictors.name: Level of consciousness
predictors.assess: Level of consciousness before treatment. Alert, or somnolent/confused = 0; stuporous or comatose = 1.

predictors.name: Worst seizure type
predictors.assess: The most severe seizure semiology. Simple-partial, complex-partial, absence or myoclonic = 0; generalised-convulsive = 1; non-convulsive SE in coma = 2.

predictors.name: Age
predictors.assess: Child's age. Under 2 years adds 2 points (the paediatric modification of STESS); 2 years or older adds none.

predictors.name: History of previous seizures
predictors.assess: Any known history of seizures before this episode. Yes = 0; no or unknown = 1.

model.panel_title: STEPSS — outcome after paediatric status epilepticus

rows.label: STEPSS 0 — favourable: very low risk of poor outcome (NPV 99% below cut-off)
STEPSS 1 — favourable: very low risk of poor outcome (NPV 99%)
STEPSS 2 — favourable: low risk of poor outcome (NPV 99%)
STEPSS 3 — favourable: low risk (below optimal cut-off >3)
STEPSS 4 — above cut-off (>3): elevated risk of poor outcome (group ≥4 PPV ~37%)
STEPSS 5 — high risk of poor outcome (group ≥5 PPV ~91%)
STEPSS 6 — high risk of poor outcome (group ≥5 PPV ~91%)

model.note: Risk of an unfavourable outcome (POPC ≥3) in children with SE. STEPSS = STESS with the age rule changed to <2 years = 2 points (Sidharth 2019, Table 1). Optimal cut-off >3 (≥4): sensitivity 93% / specificity 81%, NPV 99%, AUC 0.95. Per-score bands are derived from the published cut-off operating characteristics (Table 3: ≥4 PPV 37%, ≥5 PPV 91%), NOT exact per-point risks — its strength is ruling OUT a poor outcome (high NPV).
model.viz_info: Consciousness, worst seizure type, age (<2 y) and seizure history each add points (waterfall). A total of 0–3 is favourable (very high NPV for a good outcome); ≥4 is the validated threshold for an elevated risk of poor outcome, rising steeply at ≥5. The donut shows the band's risk of a poor outcome.

provenance.source: STEPSS point table taken verbatim from Sidharth et al., Seizure 2019, Table 1 (consciousness 0/1; worst seizure type 0/1/2; age <2 y = 2 else 0; history of previous seizures yes 0 / no-or-unknown 1; total 0–6). Cut-off operating characteristics from Table 3 (>3: sens 0.93/spec 0.81/NPV 0.99; ≥4 PPV 0.37; ≥5 PPV 0.91; AUC 0.95). PDF in 0_inbox/papers/status_epilepticus/.
provenance.notes: Score kind, 4 predictors, total 0–6. Only the age criterion differs from STESS (<2 y = 2 pts). STEPSS validates a cut-off (>3) not per-score risk, so rows carry band risks derived from Table 3 PPVs (0–3 favourable ~1–3% via NPV 99%; 4 → group-≥4 PPV 37%; 5–6 → group-≥5 PPV 91%) — flagged approximate in note/cff_info. Paediatric (1 mo–18 y). Fills the site's paediatric-SE gap.
